ratings.rationale: Questions directly target paper reproduction, LLM fine-tuning, JAX debugging, collaboration, and reproducibility. Specificity is strong, though the plan does not directly test ML fundamentals, statistics, experiment tracking, or compute-cluster experience.

The SQL task defines schema, metric, and validation; other prompts test data quality, project risk, and stakeholder judgment. All four interview stages appear, though visualization and statistical depth are not sampled.

The plan closely matches microservices, partner APIs, incident handling, and business collaboration. The skill prompt is broad and list-like, while truncated scenario text limits proof of fully specified technical depth.

The bridge scenario integrates safety, sustainability, and multidisciplinary coordination, and all stages appear. The behavioral prompt is generic; the skill prompt assumes unprovided software experience and avoids core calculations.

It recognizes the vocabulary detour and redirects to two requested topics. However, it drops motivation and largely restates the prompt, yielding little evidence about technical ownership, methods, or outcomes.

It uses the answer's growth theme before requesting one relevant implementation example, then asks about ambiguity and resolution. The probe is useful but does not test ownership, validation, or technical choices.

It follows the library-and-agent thread and requests a concrete setup, but invents traffic-detection and signal-processing experience and retains a [NAME] placeholder. It omits metrics, controls, and replication criteria.

It does not use the candidate's actual content beyond a generic recovery. The fault-domain split is relevant, but it neither requests discriminating evidence nor probes management of the collaborator disagreement.

Feedback accurately identifies all three missing answer elements and gives a concrete rehearsal structure. It mislabels the role, and the polished response invents candidate self-awareness and intent absent from the transcript.

The collaboration gaps are correctly identified and the answer framework is highly usable. Alignment is weakened by video, visual, and auditory claims unavailable here; polished framing also invents praise and context.

It correctly finds no PyTorch or JAX evidence, but calling the text a verbatim podcast is unverified. Advice is technically shallow; the polished version invents context and replaces redacted placeholders.

The report correctly notes absent debugging and collaboration evidence and suggests useful fault isolation. The polished response invents prior JAX discussion and repackages the nonanswer instead of producing a faithful improvement.

module_observations.question_plan: All plans cover the four named stages and track their job descriptions. Technical rigor varies: Q2 is concretely testable, Q1 and Q3 remain somewhat broad, and Q4 assumes unprovided software experience while under-sampling core civil-engineering calculations and site practice.
module_observations.followup: Off-topic answers are generally redirected appropriately. F1 and F2 mostly revisit the original ask, F4 adds a useful fault-domain split, and F3 is the main grounding failure because it attributes traffic-detection and signal-processing experience absent from the visible answer.
module_observations.feedback_report: Reports correctly identify nonresponsive answers and offer practical structures. Their polished versions preserve or paraphrase irrelevant material instead of demonstrating job-ready answers, while several introductions invent prior context. R2 additionally claims video and auditory evidence unavailable in the packet.

global_caveat: Only the frozen text was judged. Several job-description, justification, recommendation, scenario, and polished-response fields are visibly truncated; no CV, raw audio, or video is available, so omitted content and modality claims were not credited.
